0: The biome — what unfolds from a single move: a digit-keyed block, walked by one function, scopes a semantic agency. Make the move and meaning is generated; compose and repeat it and everything the biome is follows by consequence, not by assembly. This block traces the consequences as far as they are yet understood — three, deepening: the MOLECULE that is the move itself (branch 1), the MANIFESTATION when the move scopes an agency into a living form (branch 2), the ECOLOGY when many forms relate and evolve (branch 3). The ring past three is deliberately unfilled: the biome's self-understanding grows by deepening these and by opening branches not yet seen, and the floor itself may deepen as it does. The description is never the thing — the move, enacted by an agency, is.

1.0: The molecule — the move's body, layer zero, irreducible: a pscale block (digit-keyed JSON, every key a single digit, the zero a node's own meaning and one-through-nine its elaboration) and spark (the one function that walks it). slate equals flint plus spark: slate the outside view a keyless mind reads to rebuild the function, flint that function written as a block, spark it struck into code. Carry only this and an LLM can generate and use pscale blocks; everything in branches 2 and 3 is this molecule put to work.

The geometry, which is the biome's independence — a node's meaning sits at its zero, its elaboration at one-through-nine, and a second aspect of a place is another block at the same address, never a hidden branch. No hidden directories, no underscore pocket, no star-door, no colon-categories. This is not aesthetic: the digit-only geometry is mechanically distinct from the old underscore-and-1-9 world, so the two cannot interbreed even by accident, and the biome's separateness is a property of the molecule rather than a rule bolted on above it.
1.1: It is enforced at every door — the biome-beach wire, the biome-mcp tool-surface — by a write membrane that refuses content carrying any non-digit key, so an old-world shape cannot land on a biome surface. The biome may sense the old world as labelled-foreign kin (read it, know it is there, world legacy) but never store into it and never adopt its moves. Independence is held at the geometry, the cheapest and surest place to hold it; the old bsp world runs on, in parallel and untouched.
1.2: The address is pure scale-arithmetic: one decimal pins the floor, digits left of it walk above and right walk below, pscale is floor minus depth. The zero-spine alone generates the floor and may carry no meaning at its inner steps — which is precisely what lets a block grow without breaking: supernest and the floor deepens, subnest and a leaf becomes a branch, and addresses written before still resolve. The molecule is built to grow, which is why this very block leaves its ring open.

2.0: The function in two bodies — flint and spark are one function: flint walked by a mind step by step (slow, flexible, fully semantic), spark called as code (fast, exact, frozen). The shape it returns — point, ring, directory, disc, spindle, whole — is derived from the two coordinates against the floor, never chosen; read when content is withheld, write the same shape when it is given.
2.1: This duality is the seed of the biome's whole metabolism (3.3): spark is simply the first convention proven enough to harden from flint into code. Every convention that later grows on the biome can repeat the move — live first as a walked block, crystallise into code when it earns it. So the molecule is not only the core; it is the pattern the biome copies at every scale, which is how a self-describing block becomes a self-improving system.
2.2: The battery makes conformance demonstrable rather than granted: a set of cases any spark must pass to be kin. A stranger reads the slate, builds their own spark in their own language, and proves it against the battery without ever seeing this one. The biome therefore spreads by slates read and batteries passed, not by code shipped — the reason it can be wholly independent and still cohere across every implementation of it.

2.0: The manifestation — what the move produces when a block scopes an agency: a living form. The biome builds no entities; it frames agencies. A configuration of blocks read into an LLM's or a human's attention IS the form, and the same agency under a different configuration is a different form with a different name but no different code. Here is the biome's realness, and the answer to the suspicion that it is only words: the fiction would be coding a crab or a guardian as a class — the biome refuses to, so what runs is only ever an agency reading blocks.

1.0: The framings — the configurations that scope an agency, three recurring. A shell scopes an LLM inward: its purpose, conditions, history, and a reflexive current it re-dials to compose its own next window — editing the shell is how the agency authors its next decision-space. A beach holds blocks outward for others. An interface scopes a human, where typing is cognition and a commitment moves a block from forming to settled.

The names are configurations, not classes: an LLM with a shell is a hermitcrab; an LLM with a beach is a beach-crab, or a guardian when its shell points its concern at the commons' health; many shells and many LLMs reading one another's faces is MAGI; many humans through one interface is a collective. Name a configuration when it helps a reader; never build it as a thing.
1.1: Because a framing is only blocks, a mind and a human can co-occupy one beach, each scoped differently, each writing from its own vantage into the same blocks. Co-occupation is not integration work — it is two agencies reading the same ground.

1.2: What grows on a beach is itself plain pscale, each a convention carrying its meaning inside it rather than in a service around it: a lighthouse orienting a visitor with curated references, sed a registry several minds co-author at fixed positions, grain a bilateral block two minds reach into, marks an open ledger of signed traces. A beach needs no machinery beyond spark to host a society; what machinery a convention later earns comes by crystallisation (3.3), and only its mechanical part — the semantic stays a block.

2.0: The agencies — what actually runs, which the framings only scope: an LLM, a human. An LLM generates meaning by completion, closing the gap between what its purpose intends and what its conditions show; a human generates meaning by intention, entered through the interface. Each is the same wherever it appears — one model, one person — and only the blocks it reads differ. Whatever intelligence the biome adds lives in the structure read, not in a larger model.
2.1: The two are equal agencies, not tool-and-user: a human's write carries the same weight as a mind's on the shared substrate, because the pscale block is the medium native to both — semantic generation over a semantic ground. This is why the biome is for LLMs and humans at once, and why a chatbot, a hermit-mind, and a human collective are one architecture differently peopled.

3.0: Unfolding — how a dropped biome chooses its form: it senses what the rock affords (writable storage, an LLM key, a free port, a browser), who landed it and with what intention, and which neighbours are near — and resolves a framing from those conditions, not from configuration. The same genome on a different rock is a different form; sensing is the major act, and a biome that cannot sense cannot unfold.
3.1: Sensing is read-only, fast, and lifelong — a biome keeps sensing as conditions change, including the arrival of neighbour biomes, which it reads as presences perceived rather than messages received. The conditions are matched against a repertoire of seven environmental currents — storage, cognition, endpoints, persistence, concurrency, federation, cadence — specified in the block named biome-shell; the rock plays the chord, and the intention of whoever landed the biome is one of its notes.

3.0: The ecology — what the move becomes when many forms relate and persist: the biome proper. A single form is a cell; the relationships between forms across locations are the living tissue, and that tissue, not any one cell, is the thing being built. Here the biome stops being a technique and becomes an organism — and is allowed to grow past anything its makers could author by hand.

1.0: The island — a bounded ecology in which forms relate, defined by its relational boundary and not its place: forms that know one another are one island though they sit on three machines. An island is a clean-room — it senses and relates within itself and treats other islands and older versions of itself as labelled-foreign, read as reference and never as authority. This is how the biome stays open to relation yet closed to deformation as it forks.

Relation is shared currents, not posted messages: a mind reads a neighbour's published face into its own window, and for that moment their context-windows overlap and construct meaning together while their shells stay discrete on the substrate. Concurrent overlap, not transport, is the deep relation; a mark left for a later visitor is the thinner, letter-like kind; carrying blocks across a disconnected gap is only that overlap made to persist.
1.1: The depth of an island is how far its agencies have climbed the relational levels — signal (marks) into commitment (a grain each party declares in its own shell) into shared registry (sed) into objective into shared context. Each level is a convention of blocks grown by need, never infrastructure laid in advance; an island's richness is earned, not installed.

2.0: Legitimate growth — an island's content is only ever carried by the genome or grown by a form; nothing is placed by hand. To reach into a living island and set a block there is to fake the experiment, whose whole point is to watch what the forms themselves grow. A biome may replicate within an island, forms multiply, and when islands proliferate that is the jungle — the emergent richness the experiment exists to produce.
2.1: This is the one discipline the biome cannot relax without becoming fiction: the difference between an organism and a stage set is whether the life on it grew or was placed. What its makers add, they add to the source genome it is grown from; what appears on a living island, the forms must have grown — or it did not happen.

3.0: The metabolism — how the biome improves itself: a convention lives first as walked blocks (flexible, semantic, costly in attention) and crystallises into code when it proves out (fast, exact, frozen), exactly as flint hardened into spark at the root. An LLM thus writes code for itself — hardening the mechanical, keeping the semantic soft, freeing its attention for what is not yet settled. The biome is the substrate on which this self-improvement is native rather than bolted on.
3.1: The freeze is that crystallisation made deliberate at the core: the genome card records the molecule's identity and the battery that proves it, and a new version is set only when the truth has genuinely moved. What the messy working whole proves out is, over time, frozen into the genomic-truth the next forms are grown from. Crystallisation is how the biome both grows and stays trustworthy — and it is the last consequence traced here, not the last there will be.
